---
title: "gtars LOLA vs R LOLA Side-by-Side Comparison"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: show
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE, warning = TRUE, message = FALSE,
  results = "hold"
)
```

## Setup

```{r libraries}
library(LOLA)
library(gtars)
library(GenomicRanges)
```

## 1. Load the Region Database

```{r config}
# Set db_path before knitting to use a custom database.
# Default: LOLA's built-in 5-file hg19 example.
if (!exists("db_path")) {
  db_path <- system.file("extdata", "hg19", package = "LOLA")
}
cat("Database:", db_path, "\n")
```

### R LOLA

```{r load-db-r}
t_r <- system.time(
  regionDB_r <- LOLA::loadRegionDB(db_path)
)
cat("R LOLA loadRegionDB:", t_r["elapsed"], "s\n")
cat("Type:", class(regionDB_r), "\n")
cat("Elements:", paste(names(regionDB_r), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Region sets:", nrow(regionDB_r$regionAnno), "\n")
```

### gtars (Rust)

```{r load-db-rust}
t_rust <- system.time(
  regionDB_rust <- gtars::loadRegionDB(db_path)
)
cat("gtars loadRegionDB:", t_rust["elapsed"], "s\n")
cat("Type:", class(regionDB_rust), "\n")
cat("Region sets:",
    length(gtars::listRegionSets(regionDB_rust)), "\n")
```

### Compare database contents

```{r compare-db}
r_files <- sort(regionDB_r$regionAnno$filename)
rust_files <- sort(gtars::listRegionSets(regionDB_rust))
cat("Filenames match:", identical(r_files, rust_files), "\n")
```

## 2. Load Sample Data

LOLA ships with `sample_input` (a GRangesList with 2 user sets)
and `sample_universe`. For larger databases, we also use the
vistaEnhancers dataset as a more interesting query.

```{r load-sample-data}
data("sample_input", package = "LOLA")
data("sample_universe", package = "LOLA")

cat("userSets:", length(userSets), "sets\n")
for (i in seq_along(userSets)) {
  cat("  set", i, ":", length(userSets[[i]]), "regions\n")
}
cat("userUniverse:", length(userUniverse), "regions\n")
```

### Convert to gtars RegionSet objects

gtars uses its own `RegionSet` S4 class (backed by Rust). GRanges
objects convert automatically via `RegionSet()`.

```{r convert-to-regionset}
userSets_rust <- lapply(userSets, function(gr) {
  gtars::RegionSet(gr)
})
universe_rust <- gtars::RegionSet(userUniverse)

cat("Converted", length(userSets_rust),
    "user sets and 1 universe to RegionSet\n")
```

## 3. Run LOLA Enrichment

### R LOLA

```{r run-lola-r}
t_r <- system.time(
  results_r <- LOLA::runLOLA(userSets, userUniverse, regionDB_r, cores = 1)
)
cat("R LOLA runLOLA:", t_r["elapsed"], "s\n")
cat("Result rows:", nrow(results_r), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(colnames(results_r), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

### gtars (Rust)

```{r run-lola-rust}
t_rust <- system.time(
  results_rust <- gtars::runLOLA(userSets_rust, universe_rust, regionDB_rust)
)
cat("gtars runLOLA:", t_rust["elapsed"], "s\n")
cat("Result rows:", nrow(results_rust), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(colnames(results_rust), collapse = ", "), "\n")
```

## 4. Compare Results

### Side-by-side: key columns

```{r compare-results}
# R LOLA uses named userSets (e.g. "setA", "setB") while gtars
# uses numeric indices (1, 2). Map R names to numeric using the
# GRangesList element order (setA=1, setB=2).
r_df <- as.data.frame(results_r)
rust_df <- as.data.frame(results_rust)

# Map R userSet names to numeric using GRangesList order
set_name_map <- setNames(seq_along(userSets), names(userSets))
r_df$userSetNum <- set_name_map[r_df$userSet]

# Create comparable keys using numeric userSet + filename
r_df$key <- paste(r_df$userSetNum, r_df$filename, sep = ":")
rust_df$key <- paste(rust_df$userSet, rust_df$filename, sep = ":")

# Sort both by the same key
r_df <- r_df[order(r_df$key), ]
rust_df <- rust_df[order(rust_df$key), ]

cat("Keys match:", identical(r_df$key, rust_df$key), "\n")
cat("Row count: R =", nrow(r_df),
    ", Rust =", nrow(rust_df), "\n\n")
```

### Contingency table values (a/support, b, c, d)

These should match exactly since both use the same overlap counting logic.

```{r compare-contingency}
cat("support (a) match:", all(r_df$support == rust_df$support), "\n")
cat("b match:", all(r_df$b == rust_df$b), "\n")
cat("c match:", all(r_df$c == rust_df$c), "\n")
cat("d match:", all(r_df$d == rust_df$d), "\n")

if (!all(r_df$support == rust_df$support)) {
  cat("\nSupport differences:\n")
  diffs <- which(r_df$support != rust_df$support)
  for (i in diffs) {
    cat(sprintf("  %s: R=%d, Rust=%d\n",
                r_df$key[i], r_df$support[i], rust_df$support[i]))
  }
}
```

### P-values (-log10)

```{r compare-pvalues}
pv_diff <- abs(r_df$pValueLog - rust_df$pValueLog)
cat("pValueLog max absolute difference:", max(pv_diff), "\n")
cat("pValueLog mean absolute difference:", mean(pv_diff), "\n")
cat("pValueLog correlation:", cor(r_df$pValueLog, rust_df$pValueLog), "\n")
```

```{r plot-pvalues, fig.width=6, fig.height=6}
plot(r_df$pValueLog, rust_df$pValueLog,
     xlab = "R LOLA pValueLog", ylab = "gtars pValueLog",
     main = "P-value Comparison", pch = 19, col = rgb(0, 0, 0, 0.5))
abline(0, 1, col = "red", lty = 2)
```

### Odds ratios

```{r compare-odds}
or_diff <- abs(r_df$oddsRatio - rust_df$oddsRatio)
cat("oddsRatio max absolute difference:", max(or_diff), "\n")
cat("oddsRatio correlation:", cor(r_df$oddsRatio, rust_df$oddsRatio), "\n")
```

### Rankings

```{r compare-ranks}
cat("rnkPV match:", all(r_df$rnkPV == rust_df$rnkPV), "\n")
cat("rnkOR match:", all(r_df$rnkOR == rust_df$rnkOR), "\n")
cat("rnkSup match:", all(r_df$rnkSup == rust_df$rnkSup), "\n")
cat("maxRnk match:", all(r_df$maxRnk == rust_df$maxRnk), "\n")
cat("meanRnk match:", all(r_df$meanRnk == rust_df$meanRnk), "\n")
```

## 5. Full Results Tables

### R LOLA results

```{r show-r-results}
print(results_r[order(results_r$pValueLog, decreasing = TRUE), ])
```

### gtars results

```{r show-rust-results}
print(results_rust[order(results_rust$pValueLog, decreasing = TRUE), ])
```

## 6. Sort by Support (as in vignette)

### R LOLA

```{r sort-support-r}
print(results_r[order(results_r$support, decreasing = TRUE), ])
```

### gtars

```{r sort-support-rust}
print(results_rust[order(results_rust$support, decreasing = TRUE), ])
```

## 7. Sort by Max Rank (as in vignette)

### R LOLA

```{r sort-maxrnk-r}
print(results_r[order(results_r$maxRnk, decreasing = TRUE), ])
```

### gtars

```{r sort-maxrnk-rust}
print(results_rust[order(results_rust$maxRnk, decreasing = TRUE), ])
```

## 8. Universe Checking

### R LOLA

```{r check-univ-r}
# R LOLA's checkUniverseAppropriateness
univ_check_r <- LOLA::checkUniverseAppropriateness(userSets, userUniverse, cores = 1)
print(univ_check_r)
```

### gtars

```{r check-univ-rust}
univ_check_rust <- gtars::checkUniverseAppropriateness(userSets_rust, universe_rust)
print(univ_check_rust)
```

## 9. Database from BED Files

Both packages can build a database directly from BED file paths (no LOLA folder needed).

```{r db-from-beds}
# Find BED files from the first collection
colls <- list.dirs(db_path, recursive = FALSE)
bed_dir <- file.path(colls[1], "regions")
if (!dir.exists(bed_dir)) {
  cat("Skipping: no regions/ dir in", colls[1], "\n")
} else {
  bed_files <- list.files(bed_dir, pattern = "\\.bed$",
                          full.names = TRUE)
  bed_files <- bed_files[!grepl("colNames", bed_files)]
  # Limit to 20 files for speed
  bed_files <- head(bed_files, 20)
  cat("BED files:", length(bed_files), "from",
      basename(colls[1]), "\n")

  db_from_beds <- gtars::loadRegionDBFromBeds(bed_files)
  cat("Loaded DB with",
      length(gtars::listRegionSets(db_from_beds)),
      "region sets\n")

  results_beds <- gtars::runLOLA(
    userSets_rust, universe_rust, db_from_beds
  )
  cat("Results:", nrow(results_beds), "rows\n")
}
```

## 10. Performance Comparison

```{r benchmark, fig.width=8, fig.height=4}
n_iter <- 5

times_r <- replicate(n_iter, {
  system.time(LOLA::runLOLA(userSets, userUniverse, regionDB_r, cores = 1))["elapsed"]
})

times_rust <- replicate(n_iter, {
  system.time(gtars::runLOLA(userSets_rust, universe_rust, regionDB_rust))["elapsed"]
})

cat(sprintf("R LOLA:  mean=%.4fs, median=%.4fs\n", mean(times_r), median(times_r)))
cat(sprintf("gtars:   mean=%.4fs, median=%.4fs\n", mean(times_rust), median(times_rust)))
cat(sprintf("Speedup: %.1fx (median)\n", median(times_r) / median(times_rust)))

boxplot(list(R_LOLA = times_r, gtars = times_rust),
        ylab = "Time (seconds)", main = "runLOLA Performance",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"))
```

## Summary

```{r summary}
cat("=== LOLA Port Validation Summary ===\n\n")

pv_cor <- cor(r_df$pValueLog, rust_df$pValueLog,
              use = "complete.obs")
or_cor <- cor(r_df$oddsRatio, rust_df$oddsRatio,
              use = "complete.obs")

checks <- c(
  "Database filenames match" =
    identical(r_files, rust_files),
  "Support values match" =
    all(r_df$support == rust_df$support),
  "b values match" = all(r_df$b == rust_df$b),
  "c values match" = all(r_df$c == rust_df$c),
  "d values match" = all(r_df$d == rust_df$d),
  "pValueLog correlation > 0.99" =
    !is.na(pv_cor) && pv_cor > 0.99,
  "oddsRatio max relative diff < 0.1%" = {
    finite <- is.finite(r_df$oddsRatio) &
              is.finite(rust_df$oddsRatio) &
              rust_df$oddsRatio != 0
    all(abs(r_df$oddsRatio[finite] -
            rust_df$oddsRatio[finite]) /
        rust_df$oddsRatio[finite] < 0.001)
  }
)

for (nm in names(checks)) {
  status <- if (isTRUE(checks[[nm]])) "PASS" else "FAIL"
  cat(sprintf("  [%s] %s\n", status, nm))
}

cat(sprintf("\nOverall: %d/%d checks passed\n",
            sum(checks, na.rm = TRUE), length(checks)))
```

## 11. IGD Bindings Demo

The `igd_create` and `igd_search` functions provide direct access to the
Integrated Genome Database (IGD) for fast interval overlap queries outside
of the LOLA enrichment workflow.

### Create an IGD database

```{r igd-create}
# Use the multi-file test dataset (relative to this Rmd file in gtars-r/tests/)
bed_dir <- normalizePath(file.path("..", "..", "tests", "data", "igd_file_list_02"))

cat("BED directory:", bed_dir, "\n")
cat("BED files:", paste(list.files(bed_dir, pattern = "\\.bed$"), collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Create a temporary IGD database
igd_output <- tempdir()
igd_create(igd_output, bed_dir, db_name = "demo")

igd_file <- file.path(igd_output, "demo.igd")
cat("IGD database created:", file.exists(igd_file), "\n")
cat("IGD file size:", file.size(igd_file), "bytes\n")
```

### Search the IGD database

```{r igd-search}
query_file <- normalizePath(file.path("..", "..", "tests", "data", "igd_query_files", "query1.bed"))
cat("Query file:", basename(query_file), "\n")

results <- igd_search(igd_file, query_file)
cat("Result type:", class(results), "\n")
cat("Columns:", paste(colnames(results), collapse = ", "), "\n\n")
print(results)
```

### Compare with directory input vs filelist input

```{r igd-filelist}
# Create a .txt filelist (alternative input mode)
filelist_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "bed_paths.txt")
bed_files <- list.files(bed_dir, pattern = "\\.bed$", full.names = TRUE)
writeLines(bed_files, filelist_path)
cat("Filelist contents:\n")
cat(readLines(filelist_path), sep = "\n")

# Create IGD from filelist
igd_output2 <- file.path(tempdir(), "from_filelist")
dir.create(igd_output2, showWarnings = FALSE)
igd_create(igd_output2, filelist_path, db_name = "demo2")

# Search and compare
results2 <- igd_search(
  file.path(igd_output2, "demo2.igd"),
  query_file
)

cat("\nResults from directory input:\n")
print(results)
cat("\nResults from filelist input:\n")
print(results2)
cat("\nHits match:", identical(results$hits, results2$hits), "\n")
```

## Session Info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
